description.overview: The Lakeland Terrier was bred to protect sheep from foxes in England's Lake District.
description.temperament: Lakelands are bold, confident, and friendly. They're independent but less scrappy than some terriers.
description.health: Generally healthy. May be prone to eye problems and Legg-Calve-Perthes disease.
description.exercise: Moderate to high energy. Need daily exercise and mental stimulation.

verdict.summary: Bold, confident terriers from England's Lake District. Lakelands are friendly and hypoallergenic but have typical terrier independence.
